article_title: Figure AI Says Its BotQ Factory Now Builds One Humanoid Per Hour, a 24x Ramp in Under 120 Days
summary: Figure AI BotQ throughput claim — every number, name, and direct quote verified against all 4 snapshots (Figure's own production update, Figure's BotQ post, Interesting Engineering corroboration, PR Newswire Series C release). Allowlist warning is non-substantive.

content_preview.title: Figure AI Says Its BotQ Factory Now Builds One Humanoid Per Hour, a 24x Ramp in Under 120 Days
content_preview.summary: Figure AI reports its California BotQ line scaled from one Figure 03 per day to one per hour in under 120 days, shipping over 350 robots with 80% first-pass yield.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Figure AI says its BotQ manufacturing facility in California has scaled from producing one Figure 03 humanoid per day to one per hour over the past 120 days, a 24-fold throughput increase that the company describes as the operational backbone for its plan to ship tens of thousands of humanoid robots a year. The announcement, posted on the company's blog on April 29, 2026, comes alongside an update to Figure's Helix AI stack that lets the robot navigate stairs and uneven terrain stra...

recommendations: Add figure.ai to config/source_allowlist.txt — primary source for an active humanoid-robotics manufacturer that the bot is likely to cite repeatedly.

editor_notes.content_quality: Mid-length News piece (929 words). Strong structure: Overview / What Figure Disclosed / A New Locomotion Capability, Trained Entirely in Simulation / Context: A \$39 Billion Valuation Behind the Ramp / What We Don't Know / Why It Matters. Heavy attribution density on every numeric claim. Notable: the 'What We Don't Know' section critically flags self-reported yield numbers and the 80% vs >99% mature-electronics benchmark gap — that's honest editorial caveating, not editorializing.
editor_notes.source_verification: 4 sources, all snapshots HTTP 200. source-0 (Interesting Engineering): confirms 24x throughput / 120 days / 350+ robots / 9,000 actuators / 12,000 robots/year ceiling, Helix System 0 / perception-conditioned / stereo cameras framing, 150 networked workstations / 80 functional verification tests, stress-and-burn-in 'squats and jogging' phrasing. source-1 (Figure 'ramping' blog post): confirms verbatim 'from 1 Figure 03 per day to 1 per hour - a 24x throughput improvement in under 120 days'; 'over 350 of our third generation humanoid robots'; 'over 9,000 actuators across more than 10 distinct SKUs'; 'over 500 battery packs'; '99.3%' battery yield; 'now over 80% and improving weekly' end-of-line yield; '80 functional verification tests'; '150 networked workstations'; '50 in-process inspection points'; 'perception-conditioned whole-body control'; 'zero-shot - no real-world fine-tuning'; 'The same network weights that learn to climb procedurally generated staircases in sim now traverse real-world stairs on the robot'. source-2 (Figure BotQ post): confirms '12,000 robots per year' first-gen line rating, 'can easily scale to 100,000 robots or 3,000,000 actuators in the next four years', Figure 03 as 'our production robot built for affordability and high-volume manufacturing', plan to use Figure robots in its own manufacturing. source-3 (PR Newswire Series C): confirms 'exceeded more than \$1 billion in committed capital', '\$39 billion post-money valuation', September 2025 announcement timing, Parkway Venture Capital lead, full investor list (Brookfield, NVIDIA, Macquarie, Intel Capital, Align Ventures, Tamarack Global, LG Technology Ventures, Salesforce, T-Mobile Ventures, Qualcomm Ventures), Brett Adcock quote 'This milestone is critical to unlocking the next stage of growth for humanoid robots, scaling out our AI platform Helix and BotQ manufacturing' verbatim.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every numeric claim (24x, 120 days, 350+ robots, 9,000 actuators, 500 battery packs, 99.3%, 80%, 12,000/year, 100,000/4 years, 3,000,000 actuators, 150 workstations, 80 verification tests, 50 inspection points, \$1B+ committed, \$39B post-money), every named entity (BotQ, Figure 03, Helix, System 0, Brett Adcock, Parkway, Brookfield, NVIDIA, etc.), and every direct quote trace to verified Figure or PR Newswire snapshots. Interesting Engineering provides independent corroboration of the company-claimed numbers.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral with appropriate skepticism. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately flags self-reported nature of yield numbers, 80% vs >99% mature-electronics benchmark gap, missing customer-vs-pilot breakdown of the 350 units, no independent audit, no stair-policy benchmarks against alternative locomotion approaches. The 'Why It Matters' framing ('industry has spent two years collecting demo videos') is editorial judgment that the broader market context supports.
editor_notes.originality: Distinct topic. Several prior Figure AI articles exist (2026-04-02 White House appearance, 2026-04-05 Melania Trump education summit) but those covered demonstrations and political optics, not BotQ manufacturing throughput. Adjacent to 2026-04-03 Hyundai/Atlas mass-production article (competitor coverage of the same industry transition). New manufacturing-side beat for Figure.

source_verification_per_url.notes: Confirms 24x / 120 days / 350+ / 9,000 actuators / 12,000 ceiling / Helix System 0 / perception-conditioned / stereo cameras / 150 workstations / 80 verification tests / squats-and-jogging stress.
Every cited verbatim string from Figure's production update confirmed — throughput, yield, network-weights sim-to-real, perception-conditioned whole-body control, zero-shot transfer.
Confirms 12,000 robots/year ceiling, 100,000 robots / 3,000,000 actuators scale-up plan, 'production robot built for affordability and high-volume manufacturing' framing.
Confirms Series C details, \$1B+ / \$39B post-money, full investor list, Adcock quote verbatim.

editor_notes.verdict_override_rationale: Auto-verdict APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS triggered solely by allowlist warning on figure.ai (the company's own primary site, used twice). No content issue, no fabricated quote, no misattribution. Overriding to APPROVE per the false-positive override path.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean APPROVE. Strong manufacturing-side reporting with comprehensive primary-source attribution and honest editorial caveating on self-reported yield numbers.
